subject: Computer Networking
title: Hashing: Digital Fingerprints
description: Learn Hashing: Digital Fingerprints in one minute.

hook.text: Change one letter in a document and the hash changes completely. That's how you know data hasn't been tampered with.

buildup.text: A hash function takes any input and produces a fixed-size output. SHA-256 always gives a 256-bit fingerprint.

discovery.text: Hashing is one-way. You can't reverse the hash to get the original data. That makes it perfect for passwords.

twist.text: Two inputs producing the same hash is a collision. Good hash functions make collisions astronomically unlikely.

climax.text: Blockchains, password storage, file integrity checks, and digital signatures all depend on reliable hashing.

punchline.text: A hash proves data is unchanged. One bit different, totally different hash.

quiz.question: What property makes hash functions useful for password storage?
options: They are one-way — you can't reverse them to get the original input
They compress passwords to save storage space
They encrypt passwords with a secret key
